scenario: Wren Retailer runs Camunda 8.8 with 8 Spring Boot services handling 6500 orders per hour and still sets camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout in its YAML. The platform team must adopt the documented 8.8 replacement key before retiring the legacy entry.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout with camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout under the unified worker namespace.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout with camunda.client.worker.override.timeout under the unified worker namespace.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout with camunda.client.timeout under the unified worker namespace.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout with camunda.client.defaults.timeout under the unified worker namespace.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe.defaults section maps timeout to camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout as the documented 8.8 replacement key.

b.text: Incorrect. Override entries live under the job-type key; the defaults timeout migrates to camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout instead.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no flat camunda.client.timeout entry; the replacement keeps the worker.defaults path for the job timeout.

d.text: Incorrect. The camunda.client.defaults prefix is not in the 8.8 reference; the documented home is camunda.client.worker.defaults.

explanation: camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.timeout migrates to camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout in 8.8. The property controls how long an activated job is exclusively assigned to the worker.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
